Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Class SoftSwitches

Namespace
Pandowdy.EmuCore
Assembly
Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll

Manages all Apple IIe soft switches and notifies responders when switches change.

public sealed class SoftSwitches
Inheritance
object
SoftSwitches
Inherited Members
object.Equals(object)
object.Equals(object, object)
object.GetHashCode()
object.GetType()
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Remarks

Responder Pattern: Components register as ISoftSwitchResponder to receive notifications when switches change. This decouples switch management from the components that react to changes (MemoryPool, video renderer, etc.).

Switch Categories: Memory Mapping (RAMRD, RAMWRT, ALTZP, 80STORE, BANK1), Video Mode (TEXT, MIXED, HIRES, PAGE2, 80VID, ALTCHAR), ROM Selection (INTCXROM, SLOTC3ROM), Annunciators (AN0-AN3).

Constructors

SoftSwitches()

Initializes a new instance of the SoftSwitches class with all switches set to their default states.

public SoftSwitches()

Remarks

All switches are initialized to false (off) except INTCXROM which defaults to true. This matches the Apple IIe power-on state where internal ROMs are enabled by default.

Methods

AddResponder(ISoftSwitchResponder)

Registers a component to receive notifications when soft switches change.

public void AddResponder(ISoftSwitchResponder responder)

Parameters

responder ISoftSwitchResponder

The responder to add. Must implement ISoftSwitchResponder.

Remarks

Responders receive immediate callbacks when switches change via the TriggerResponder(SoftSwitchId, bool) method. Typical responders include MemoryPool (for memory remapping) and video renderers (for display mode changes).

DumpSoftSwitchStatus(string)

Writes the current state of all soft switches to the debug output.

public void DumpSoftSwitchStatus(string header = "")

Parameters

header string

Optional header text to display before the switch status list. If provided, each switch status line will be indented.

Remarks

Output format: "SwitchName: On/Off (Changes: count)" Useful for debugging switch state during emulation and tracking switch activity patterns.

Get(SoftSwitchId)

Retrieves the current state of a specific soft switch.

public bool Get(SoftSwitches.SoftSwitchId id)

Parameters

id SoftSwitches.SoftSwitchId

The identifier of the switch to query.

Returns

bool

True if the switch is on (enabled), false if off (disabled) or not found.

GetSwitchList()

Returns a snapshot of all switches with their current state and change counts.

public List<(SoftSwitches.SoftSwitchId id, bool value)> GetSwitchList()

Returns

List<(SoftSwitches.SoftSwitchId id, bool value)>

A list of tuples containing switch ID and current valuefor each switch.

Remarks

Useful for diagnostics, save state serialization, and UI display of switch status. The change count indicates how many times each switch has toggled since reset.

ResetAllSwitches()

Resets all soft switches to their default power-on state.

public void ResetAllSwitches()

Remarks

Default state: All switches off except INTCXROM which is on (matching Apple IIe power-on).

All registered responders are notified of the state changes to ensure memory mappings and video modes are properly initialized.

Set(SoftSwitchId, bool)

Sets the state of a specific soft switch and notifies all registered responders.

public void Set(SoftSwitches.SoftSwitchId id, bool value)

Parameters

id SoftSwitches.SoftSwitchId

The identifier of the switch to modify.

value bool

The new state for the switch (true = on, false = off).

Remarks

This method updates the switch state and immediately triggers responder callbacks, which may cause memory remapping or video mode changes. The change counter for the switch is automatically incremented if the value changes.
